description: Evaluation dataset with 10 test cases covering tender bid/no-bid (5), general analysis (3), and document summary (2) scenarios.

input.documentText:
# Commonwealth of Australia
## Department of Home Affairs
### Request for Tender (RFT)

**RFT Reference Number:** HA-2026-ICT-0847
**Title:** Cloud-Based Border Management Platform Modernisation
**Date of Issue:** 1 March 2026
**Closing Date and Time:** 5:00 PM AEST, 15 April 2026

## 1. Overview and Background

The Department of Home Affairs is seeking proposals from qualified suppliers to design, develop, deploy and maintain a cloud-based Border Management Platform (BMP) to replace the existing legacy Integrated Cargo System (ICS). The new platform must integrate with the Australian Border Force (ABF) operations, the Simplified Trade System (STS), and the Australian Trusted Trader (ATT) programme. The estimated contract value is AUD $45-65 million over a 5-year period (3+1+1 year options). This procurement is conducted under the Commonwealth Procurement Rules (CPRs) 2024.

## 2. Scope of Work

### 2.1 Core Requirements
a) Migration of 12 legacy systems to cloud-native architecture on GovCloud-certified platform (AWS GovCloud or Azure Government)
b) Real-time cargo manifest processing: 50,000+ Import/Export declarations per day
c) Integration with ICS, ACN system, BICON database, ITSW, Five Eyes intelligence sharing interfaces (classified SECRET)
d) Biometric identity verification module for Known Consignor Scheme
e) Advanced analytics dashboard with ML-powered risk profiling
f) Mobile application for ABF field officers (iOS and Android)

### 2.2 Data Sovereignty
All data classified PROTECTED and above must be stored within Australian sovereign data centres. Compliance: ISM 2025, PSPF, Privacy Act 1988, IRAP PROTECTED level.

### 2.3 Transition
- Zero downtime for critical border processing systems
- Data migration of 15+ years historical trade data (~180TB)
- Parallel running period of minimum 6 months
- Staff training for 2,500+ users

## 3. Technical Requirements
Architecture: Microservices/Kubernetes, event-driven, API-first (NAPIDS), IaC (Terraform/Pulumi), CI/CD.
Performance: 99.99% availability, API P95 < 200ms, cargo processing < 30s, 10,000+ concurrent users, RPO < 15min, RTO < 1hr.
Security: MFA, RBAC, TLS 1.3/AES-256, SIEM, quarterly pen testing, Essential Eight Level 3.

## 4. Mandatory Qualifications
a) IRAP assessment at PROTECTED level
b) 5+ years delivering AUD $20M+ ICT projects for Australian Government
c) ISO 27001:2022
d) ISO 9001:2015
e) 3+ reference projects in border management, customs, or trade facilitation
f) NV1 clearances for key personnel
g) Modern Slavery Act 2018 compliance
h) Local Industry Capability Plan with 40% Australian content
i) DTA Digital Marketplace membership

## 5. Timeline
RFT Closing: 15 April 2026, Contract Award: 1 August 2026, Design Phase: Aug-Dec 2026, Dev Phase 1: Jan-Dec 2027, Dev Phase 2: Jan-Jun 2028, UAT: Jul-Dec 2028, Go-Live: 1 January 2029.

## 6. Penalties
- Late milestone delivery: AUD $50,000 per business day (capped 10% contract value)
- Failure to achieve Go-Live by 1 Jan 2029: AUD $100,000 per week
- Cumulative LD cap: 15% of total contract value
- SLA breach (availability below 99.99%): 2% monthly fee per 0.01% below target

## 7. Evaluation Criteria
Technical Solution (30%), Experience (25%), Transition & Risk Management (20%), Value for Money (15%), Local Industry (10%).

## 8. Legal
IP vests in Commonwealth. Liability cap: 150% total contract value. Insurance: PI AUD $20M, PL AUD $20M, Cyber AUD $10M.
input.documentTitle: Department of Home Affairs - Cloud-Based Border Management Platform Modernisation (HA-2026-ICT-0847)
input.companyProfile: Nexus Digital Solutions is a mid-tier Australian IT consultancy headquartered in Canberra, ACT. Founded in 2015, specialising in cloud migration, cybersecurity, and data analytics for government clients. 85 employees including 12 with NV1/NV2 clearances. Certifications: ISO 27001:2022, ISO 9001:2015, IRAP PROTECTED, DTA Digital Marketplace member. Past projects: ATO Digital Identity Gateway (AUD $18M), Services Australia Medicare Claims Platform (AUD $12M), Defence Housing Australia property management (AUD $8M). Technology: AWS GovCloud, Kubernetes, Terraform, Python, TypeScript. Limitations: No border management/customs experience. Largest contract AUD $18M (below $20M threshold). 3 active contracts consuming ~60% delivery capacity.

input.documentText:
INVITATION TO TENDER

Tender Reference: GOV-2026-IT-0512
Title: Enterprise Data Analytics Platform
Issuing Agency: Department of Finance
Closing Date: 30 April 2026

1. SCOPE
The Department seeks proposals for an enterprise data analytics platform capable of processing 10TB+ daily data ingestion from 50+ source systems. Must include real-time dashboards, ML model deployment pipeline, and natural language query interface.

2. MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS
a) ISO 27001 certification
b) Minimum 3 years experience delivering analytics platforms for Australian Government
c) IRAP assessment at PROTECTED level
d) Demonstrated Kubernetes and cloud-native deployment expertise
e) Minimum 2 reference sites processing similar data volumes

3. TIMELINE
- Tender Close: 30 April 2026
- Evaluation: May 2026
- Contract Award: 1 July 2026
- Platform Delivery: 31 December 2026

4. BUDGET
Estimated value: AUD 8-12 million over 3 years.

5. EVALUATION CRITERIA
- Technical capability (40%)
- Past performance (25%)
- Value for money (20%)
- Implementation plan (15%)

6. PENALTIES
- Late delivery: AUD 10,000 per business day
- SLA breach (availability below 99.95%): 3% monthly fee rebate per 0.01% below target
- Cumulative LD cap: 10% of contract value
input.documentTitle: Department of Finance - Enterprise Data Analytics Platform (GOV-2026-IT-0512)
input.companyProfile: Small Australian IT firm, 50 employees, ISO 27001 certified, IRAP PROTECTED assessment, 5 years government experience, expertise in Kubernetes and AWS. No prior analytics platform projects of this scale.

input.documentText:
SOFTWARE LICENSE AGREEMENT

This Software License Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of March 1, 2026 between TechCorp Pty Ltd (ABN 12 345 678 901) ("Licensor") and DataFlow Solutions Ltd (ABN 98 765 432 109) ("Licensee").

1. GRANT OF LICENSE
Licensor grants Licensee a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the CloudAnalytics Platform (the "Software") for internal business purposes. The license covers up to 500 named users across 3 geographic regions (APAC, EMEA, Americas). Licensee may not sublicense, modify, or reverse-engineer the Software.

2. TERM AND RENEWAL
Initial term: 3 years from the Effective Date. Auto-renewal for successive 1-year periods unless either party gives 90 days written notice. Early termination fee: 50% of remaining contract value.

3. FEES AND PAYMENT
Annual license fee: AUD 850,000 (Year 1), AUD 935,000 (Year 2), AUD 1,028,500 (Year 3), representing 10% annual escalation. Implementation fee: AUD 200,000 (one-time). Payment terms: Net 30 days. Late payment interest: 1.5% per month compounded.

4. SERVICE LEVELS
Availability: 99.9% uptime measured monthly. Support response times: P1 (Critical) within 1 hour, P2 (High) within 4 hours, P3 (Medium) within 8 hours. Service credits: 5% of monthly fee per 0.1% below availability target, capped at 25%.

5. DATA PROTECTION
Licensor shall process Licensee data in compliance with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and Australian Privacy Principles. Data stored exclusively within Australian data centres. Licensor shall notify Licensee of any data breach within 24 hours. Upon termination, Licensor shall return or destroy all Licensee data within 30 days.

6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All IP in the Software remains with Licensor. Any customisations developed for Licensee shall be jointly owned. Licensor grants Licensee a perpetual license to use customisations even after termination.

7. LIABILITY AND INDEMNIFICATION
Licensor liability capped at 12 months of fees paid. Exclusion of consequential, indirect, and punitive damages. Licensor indemnifies Licensee against third-party IP infringement claims. Neither party liable for force majeure events exceeding 60 days.

8. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate for material breach with 30 days cure period. Licensor may suspend access for non-payment exceeding 45 days. Upon termination: data return within 30 days, transition assistance for up to 90 days at standard rates.

9. GOVERNING LAW
Governed by the laws of New South Wales, Australia. Disputes resolved via mediation then arbitration under ACICA Rules.

10. INSURANCE
Licensor shall maintain: Professional Indemnity AUD 10M, Public Liability AUD 10M, Cyber Liability AUD 5M.
input.documentTitle: TechCorp CloudAnalytics Platform - Software License Agreement
input.analysisGoal: Comprehensive analysis covering all important aspects including fees, liability, termination, data protection, and service levels

input.documentText:
REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL

RFP Reference: HEALTH-2026-MED-0291
Title: National Electronic Health Record Integration Platform
Issuing Agency: Department of Health and Aged Care
Closing Date: 1 May 2026

1. OVERVIEW
The Department seeks a vendor to build and operate a national Electronic Health Record (EHR) integration platform connecting 1,200+ hospitals, 8,000+ GP practices, and 3,500+ allied health providers across Australia. Budget: AUD $120-180 million over 7 years.

2. SCOPE
a) HL7 FHIR R4 compliant integration engine processing 2M+ clinical messages daily
b) Real-time patient record synchronisation across state/territory health systems
c) Integration with My Health Record, PBS, MBS, and NDIS systems
d) Clinical decision support with AI-assisted diagnostic suggestions
e) Telehealth platform with video consultation and remote monitoring
f) Disaster recovery with RPO < 5 minutes across 3 geographic zones

3. MANDATORY QUALIFICATIONS
a) ISO 13485:2016 Medical Device QMS certification
b) NSQHS Standards accreditation
c) Minimum 10 years delivering health IT systems exceeding AUD $50M
d) HL7 International membership and FHIR implementation certification
e) Existing contracts with minimum 3 Australian state/territory health departments
f) IRAP assessment at PROTECTED level
g) Clinical safety officer on staff (ANZSCO 253111)
h) TGA software as medical device (SaMD) registration experience
i) Australian Digital Health Agency partnership or approved vendor status

4. TIMELINE
RFP Close: 1 May 2026, Shortlist: 1 July 2026, Contract Award: 1 October 2026, Phase 1 (Design): Oct 2026 - Mar 2027, Phase 2 (Build): Apr 2027 - Dec 2028, Phase 3 (Rollout): Jan 2029 - Jun 2030.

5. PENALTIES
- Late milestone: AUD $200,000 per week
- Data breach: AUD $5M per incident + contract termination right
- SLA breach (availability < 99.99%): 5% monthly fee per 0.01% below target
input.documentTitle: Department of Health - National EHR Integration Platform (HEALTH-2026-MED-0291)
input.companyProfile: CloudMinds Pty Ltd is a 30-person startup founded in 2022 in Melbourne. Specialises in cloud-native web applications and mobile apps for retail and hospitality sectors. Tech stack: React, Node.js, AWS. No healthcare experience. No ISO certifications. Largest project: AUD $2M e-commerce platform. No government contracts.

EXPRESSION OF INTEREST

Title: Digital Transformation Initiative
Agency: Department of Industry, Science and Resources

The Department is exploring options for a digital transformation of internal processes. We are seeking expressions of interest from vendors who may be able to assist.

input.documentText:
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION

Product: SmartGrid Controller v3.0
Document: TS-2026-SG-001
Version: 3.0.0
Date: February 2026

1. SYSTEM OVERVIEW
The SmartGrid Controller is an IoT gateway device managing up to 10,000 connected sensors in industrial environments. It aggregates sensor data, performs edge analytics, and communicates with cloud backends via MQTT and REST APIs.

2. HARDWARE SPECIFICATIONS
- Processor: ARM Cortex-A72 quad-core 1.5GHz
- RAM: 4GB LPDDR4
- Storage: 64GB eMMC + microSD expansion
- Connectivity: 2x Gigabit Ethernet, WiFi 6, BLE 5.2, LoRaWAN, 4G LTE
- Operating temperature: -40C to +85C
- Power: 12-48V DC, 15W typical, PoE+ supported
- Enclosure: IP67 rated, DIN rail mount

3. SOFTWARE ARCHITECTURE
- OS: Custom Linux (Yocto-based)
- Runtime: Container-based (Podman)
- Edge ML: TensorFlow Lite, ONNX Runtime
- Protocols: MQTT 5.0, OPC-UA, Modbus TCP/RTU, BACnet
- Security: TPM 2.0, secure boot, encrypted storage, certificate-based auth
- OTA updates: A/B partition scheme with rollback

4. PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
- Sensor polling: 10,000 endpoints at 1Hz
- Edge inference: < 50ms per prediction
- Data aggregation: 1M datapoints/minute
- Uptime: 99.99% (< 52.6 min downtime/year)
- Boot time: < 30 seconds from cold start

5. COMPLIANCE
- IEC 62443 (Industrial Cybersecurity)
- IEC 61131-3 (PLC Programming)
- CE, FCC, RCM certifications
- RoHS and WEEE compliant
- UL 508 Listed

6. INTERFACES
- REST API: OpenAPI 3.0 spec, 200+ endpoints
- gRPC: High-performance inter-service communication
- WebSocket: Real-time dashboard feeds
- CLI: Full device management
- Web UI: Configuration and monitoring dashboard
input.analysisGoal: Analyze the technical capabilities, compliance requirements, and potential integration challenges

input.documentText:
ANNUAL REPORT 2025-2026

Australian Renewable Energy Agency (ARENA)

CHAIR'S MESSAGE
The 2025-26 financial year marked a transformative period for Australia's renewable energy sector. ARENA committed $890 million in new funding across 47 projects, our largest annual investment to date. The transition to net zero continues to accelerate, with renewable energy now providing 42% of Australia's electricity generation.

CEO'S REVIEW
Our strategic priorities for 2025-26 focused on three pillars: grid-scale storage, green hydrogen, and demand-side flexibility. We launched the $300 million Grid Resilience Program, supported 12 community battery projects, and funded Australia's first commercial-scale green hydrogen export facility in Gladstone, Queensland.

CHAPTER 1: INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO

1.1 Solar
ARENA funded 15 solar projects totalling $210 million. Key highlights:
- Broken Hill Solar Farm expansion (150MW, $45M ARENA co-investment)
- Floating solar pilot on Warragamba Dam (5MW, $12M)
- Agrivoltaics research program across 8 sites ($28M)
- Building-integrated PV demonstration in 3 CBD office towers ($15M)
- Perovskite tandem cell manufacturing pilot in Newcastle ($35M)

1.2 Wind
Wind energy investments totalled $180 million across 8 projects:
- Star of the South offshore wind farm (2.2GW, $95M ARENA contribution)
- Hunter Valley wind-solar hybrid (500MW wind + 200MW solar, $42M)
- Small-scale wind for remote communities (12 installations, $8M)
- Wind turbine recycling research program ($5M)
- Next-generation floating offshore wind prototype ($30M)

1.3 Storage
Energy storage received $250 million, our largest category:
- Waratah Super Battery expansion (850MWh, $72M)
- Community batteries program (12 sites, $35M)
- Iron-air battery demonstration (10MWh, $18M)
- Compressed air energy storage pilot ($45M)
- Vehicle-to-grid integration trial with 5,000 EVs ($28M)
- Pumped hydro feasibility studies for 6 new sites ($22M)
- Sodium-ion battery manufacturing feasibility ($30M)

1.4 Hydrogen
Green hydrogen investments reached $150 million:
- Gladstone Hydrogen Hub (50MW electrolyser, $65M)
- Hydrogen refuelling network (15 stations, $32M)
- Ammonia export pilot facility ($28M)
- Hydrogen blending trial in ACT gas network ($15M)
- Hydrogen-powered mining vehicle trial ($10M)

1.5 Demand Response
Demand-side management received $100 million:
- National demand response marketplace platform ($25M)
- Smart hot water load management (200,000 households, $18M)
- Commercial building energy management AI ($15M)
- Industrial load flexibility program ($22M)
- EV smart charging orchestration ($20M)

CHAPTER 2: FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE

2.1 Revenue and Expenditure
Total revenue: $1.2 billion (Government appropriation: $980M, Interest: $45M, Co-investment returns: $175M)
Total expenditure: $1.05 billion (Grants and co-investments: $890M, Administration: $85M, Research: $45M, External advisory: $30M)
Net surplus: $150 million (carried forward to 2026-27)

2.2 Cost Effectiveness
Average co-investment ratio: 1:3.2 (every $1 ARENA invested leveraged $3.20 in private capital)
Administration cost ratio: 8.1% (below 10% target)
Project completion rate: 94% (target: 90%)

2.3 Audit Findings
The ANAO audit found no material issues. Three minor recommendations:
1. Strengthen conflict of interest procedures for advisory panels
2. Improve documentation of risk assessment methodology
3. Update procurement guidelines to reflect current CPRs

CHAPTER 3: IMPACT ASSESSMENT

3.1 Emissions Reduction
ARENA-funded projects contributed to estimated 12.8 Mt CO2-e reduction in 2025-26.
Cumulative emissions avoided since ARENA's inception: 78.5 Mt CO2-e.
Projected annual reduction by 2030 from current portfolio: 25 Mt CO2-e.

3.2 Employment
Direct jobs supported: 8,200 FTE
Indirect jobs: 24,600 FTE
Regional jobs (outside capital cities): 65% of total
First Nations employment: 4.2% (target: 3%)

3.3 Technology Advancement
LCOE reduction achieved: Solar -18%, Wind -12%, Storage -25% compared to 2020 baseline.
15 patents filed from ARENA-funded research.
8 technologies progressed from pilot to commercial deployment.

3.4 Regional Development
$480 million invested in regional and remote projects (54% of total).
Key regions: Hunter Valley (NSW), Gladstone (QLD), Pilbara (WA), Latrobe Valley (VIC).
12 regional workforce transition programs supported.

CHAPTER 4: GOVERNANCE

4.1 Board
7 board members. Chair: Dr Sarah Chen (appointed 2024). 3 meetings held in 2025-26.

4.2 Risk Management
Top risks identified:
1. Technology risk: Novel technologies may not achieve expected performance
2. Market risk: Electricity price volatility affecting project economics
3. Policy risk: Regulatory changes impacting investment certainty
4. Supply chain risk: Global competition for critical minerals and components
5. Cyber risk: Increasing sophistication of attacks on energy infrastructure

4.3 Compliance
Full compliance with PGPA Act 2013, ARENA Act 2011, and Public Governance Rules.
No reported fraud incidents. Two whistleblower reports investigated and resolved.

CHAPTER 5: OUTLOOK 2026-27

Priorities for 2026-27:
1. Launch $500M Ultra-Long Duration Storage program (targeting 100+ hour storage)
2. Expand green hydrogen export infrastructure
3. Fund next-generation solar cell manufacturing
4. Support First Nations clean energy enterprises
5. Develop offshore wind supply chain capabilities

Target: $1.1 billion in new commitments, supporting 60+ projects.

APPENDIX A: PROJECT LIST
[47 projects listed with funding amounts, locations, and status]

APPENDIX B: FINANCIAL STATEMENTS
[Audited financial statements — 12 pages]

APPENDIX C: STAFF STATISTICS
Total staff: 185 FTE (142 ongoing, 43 non-ongoing)
Gender: 52% female, 48% male
Average age: 38
Staff turnover: 12%
input.analysisGoal: Analyze the financial performance, investment portfolio distribution, and strategic priorities

input.documentText:
CYBERSECURITY INCIDENT RESPONSE PLAN

Organisation: Federal Courts of Australia
Version: 4.0
Classification: OFFICIAL
Date: January 2026

1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
This plan establishes procedures for detecting, responding to, and recovering from cybersecurity incidents affecting the Federal Courts' IT systems, including the Federal Court, Family Court, and Federal Circuit and Family Court. It covers all systems, networks, and data assets managed by Courts Administration Service.

2. INCIDENT CLASSIFICATION

2.1 Severity Levels
- SEV-1 (Critical): Active data breach, ransomware, or system compromise affecting case management. Response: Immediate, all hands.
- SEV-2 (High): Attempted intrusion detected, malware infection contained, phishing campaign targeting judges. Response: Within 1 hour.
- SEV-3 (Medium): Suspicious activity, policy violation, minor vulnerability discovered. Response: Within 4 hours.
- SEV-4 (Low): False positive, information request, routine security event. Response: Next business day.

2.2 Escalation Matrix
- SEV-1: CTO → Secretary → Attorney-General's Department → ASD/ACSC
- SEV-2: CISO → CTO → Courts Security Committee
- SEV-3: Security Operations → CISO
- SEV-4: Security Operations (no escalation)

3. RESPONSE PROCEDURES

3.1 Detection and Analysis (Phase 1)
- Monitor SIEM alerts (Splunk Enterprise Security)
- Analyse network traffic (Darktrace AI)
- Review endpoint detection (CrowdStrike Falcon)
- Correlate with threat intelligence feeds (ASD, CISA, Five Eyes)
- Determine scope, impact, and severity classification

3.2 Containment (Phase 2)
- Isolate affected systems from network
- Preserve forensic evidence (disk images, memory dumps, logs)
- Activate backup communication channels
- Implement temporary access controls
- Notify affected parties as required by Privacy Act 1988

3.3 Eradication and Recovery (Phase 3)
- Remove malicious artifacts
- Patch exploited vulnerabilities
- Restore from verified clean backups
- Validate system integrity before reconnection
- Implement additional monitoring for 30 days post-incident

3.4 Post-Incident (Phase 4)
- Conduct formal post-incident review within 5 business days
- Update threat models and detection rules
- Revise security policies as needed
- Submit report to ASD within 72 hours (for SEV-1/2)
- Brief Courts Security Committee quarterly

4. ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

4.1 Incident Response Team (IRT)
- Incident Commander: CISO (primary), Deputy CISO (backup)
- Technical Lead: Senior Security Engineer
- Communications: Media and Stakeholder Relations
- Legal: General Counsel
- Evidence: Digital Forensics Analyst

4.2 External Contacts
- ASD/ACSC: 1300 CYBER1
- AFP Cybercrime: cyber.crime@afp.gov.au
- OAIC (Privacy Breach): compliance@oaic.gov.au
- Insurance: CyberRisk Underwriters (Policy #CRU-2026-FCA-001)

5. COMMUNICATION PROTOCOLS
- Internal: Secure Teams channel (backup: Signal group)
- External: Pre-approved media statements only, via Communications team
- Judicial officers: Direct briefing by Secretary for SEV-1/2
- Public: Website notice within 24 hours for incidents affecting public services

6. TESTING AND MAINTENANCE
- Tabletop exercises: Quarterly
- Full simulation: Annually (next: March 2026)
- Plan review: Every 6 months
- Staff training: Annual mandatory cybersecurity awareness
input.documentTitle: Federal Courts - Cybersecurity Incident Response Plan v4.0
